summary.intent: Implement tasks #297, #298, #299 — the framework-agnostic Rust core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue foundation module in ars-core, as a single combined PR closing all three issues.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented and merged into PR #698 with 22+ Codex findings addressed across 5 review rounds; as of the transcript end, round 5 fixes were being pushed and the final @codex review re-triggered awaiting the 👍 before merge.

repo: ars-core is built no_std in CI (--no-default-features); use core_maths::CoreFloat for transcendental floats (powf, cbrt, sqrt, atan2) via fully-qualified syntax CoreFloat::method(x) — consistent with ars-i18n. CoreFloat lacks min/max; add private fmin/fmax helpers.
MessageFn has no generic From impl — each distinct function signature needs a hand-written From<F> impl in crates/ars-core/src/message_fn.rs. Color components needed 4 new impls.
ComponentPart derive requires Eq+Hash; AngleSlider's Part::Marker { value: f64 } needs a manual impl using to_bits() for hashing — same pattern as input/slider.

Drag machines follow the slider pattern: adapter supplies DragStart/DragMove/DragEnd events; core transitions Idle↔Dragging and applies position; on_change_end fires via a typed Effect::ChangeEnd PendingEffect on DragEnd. The spec's use_platform_effects()/track_pointer_drag() calls were nonexistent and had to be reconciled to this pattern.

Controlled-mode prop sync: Machine::on_props_changed() + SyncValue/SetProps events are required to handle live prop changes after mount; without them, controlled value/disabled/readonly go stale until remount.
Bindable::get() returns the controlled prop (not the internal/pending value); a new Bindable::pending() accessor is needed to pass the just-dragged value into on_change_end effects.

cargo xci-fast runs 10 gates; snapshot-count gate requires ≥1 insta snapshot per state variant. AngleSlider has 3 states (Idle/Dragging/Focused) and initially only 2 snapshots, causing the gate to fail.

ars-components line and branch coverage threshold is 90%; CI measures with cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace (cross-crate tests push ars-components ~1.1% higher than -p-only measurement).

ColorValue::new wraps hue into [0,360) as an invariant used by to_rgb sextant math; slider/area hue axes must track an unwrapped slider_value/x_value/y_value separately so the 360° endpoint is reachable.
spec validate checks 340 files; spec code blocks are compiled by the spec-compile-snippets gate. All spec contract changes (new events, behaviors) must be mirrored in the corresponding spec file.

code.finding: from_hex used trim_start_matches('#') which strips ALL leading # chars — ##3366ff was silently accepted. Fixed with strip_prefix('#').unwrap_or(hex). Also added is_ascii() guard to prevent panic on multi-byte chars (#ああ) when byte-slicing.

parse_rgb_args/parse_hsl_args/parse_hsb_args used str::parse::<f64>() which succeeds for NaN and inf. Added parse_finite_f64 helper that calls parse then checks is_finite(), returning None for non-finite input.

format_value shows percentage channels as integers (e.g. 50 for 0.5 saturation) but commit_input was parsing the raw number and clamping to [0,1], silently changing color. Fixed by dividing percentage-channel input by 100 before clamping.

IME composing branch (CompositionChange) ran before the readonly/disabled guards, so readonly/disabled fields accepted mid-composition edits. Added if ctx.readonly || ctx.disabled { return None } guard to the IME arm.

SetProps updated channel/color_format in Context but left ctx.input_text in the old representation. When not focused, SetProps must reformat input_text from the current value with the new channel/format.

Props::readonly only set aria-readonly; native HtmlAttr::ReadOnly was missing from input_attrs, and the machine still accepted Change/blur commits while readonly. Added native readonly attr and early-return guard in transition.

Channel mode with no value/default_value rendered role=spinbutton but had no aria-valuenow/min/max and keyboard steps were no-ops. Fixed by seeding ColorValue::default() in init when channel is Some and no value is supplied.
Hidden form input retained the last valid hex value after an invalid commit, so forms could submit stale data. Fixed by omitting the hidden value when ctx.invalid is true.

Controlled drag called ctx.value.get() for the on_change_end callback which returns the controlled prop (not the dragged value). Fixed with Bindable::pending() which returns the internal/last-set value.
Hidden form input stayed enabled when the control was disabled, submitting a value in disabled forms. Fixed by mirroring HtmlAttr::Disabled onto the hidden input in all drag/field components.

Hue x/y axis at max endpoint: with_channel wraps hue to 0 via ColorValue::new invariant, causing thumb to jump back to min. Fixed by tracking unwrapped x_value/y_value in Context as source of truth for position and ARIA readings.

aria-valuetext multiplied every channel by 100 for percentages; with x_channel=Hue this produced '18000%'. Fixed with channel-aware format_axis_reading helper (hue→°, rgb→raw, fractional→%). Also threads color_name_en() into the text per spec §3.1.

In idle state, focused_index is None so all items get tabindex=-1 — no item is reachable by Tab. Fixed with tabbable_index() helper that returns selected index (or 0) in idle, so exactly one item has tabindex=0 before focus enters.

Decrement used % which gives negative remainder; large_step > 360 from PageDown stored negative hue. Fixed with rem_euclid(360.0). Thumb also had dangling aria-labelledby pointing to a non-existent Label part — removed.

SetProps updated min/max bounds but left ctx.value unclamped, so an out-of-range value persisted in aria-valuenow and the hidden input. Fixed by re-clamping value to new [min,max] bounds in the SetProps arm.

workflow: CI's llvm-cov runs with --workspace (all crates' tests exercise ars-components), raising branch coverage ~1.1% above a -p-only local run. Always verify locally with the exact CI command before declaring coverage fixed.
Background shell tasks on macOS/zsh: 'status' is a read-only zsh special variable — use a different variable name (e.g. cov_state) in watch scripts to avoid silent crashes.
Spec code blocks that reference cross-block or external symbols should be fenced as rust,no_check to prevent spec-compile-snippets failures from drift.

The waiting-for-codex-review loop: post @codex review → watch for 👀 reaction → when 👀 drops, fetch unresolved threads → fix all with TDD → push → reply+resolve each thread → re-trigger. Codex can be rate-limited with 'reached your Codex usage limits' — retry later, quota resets on its own.
For cross-cutting changes (prop-sync events, disabled-input fixes) affecting 5-6 components, create task tracking items before implementing to ensure none are missed.
Delegating spec synchronization to a subagent with an exact change-set brief is effective for the mechanical mirroring step — keeps main context focused and runs in background while tests execute.

friction: core_maths::CoreFloat lacks min() and max() methods — required writing private fmin/fmax helpers that would otherwise be unnecessary boilerplate.
MessageFn's From<F> impl pattern has no generic mechanism — each new function arity required a separate hand-written impl, discovered only at compile time after writing the components.
The spec described non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects(), track_pointer_drag() in core) for 4 drag components, requiring significant reconciliation work to match actual codebase patterns.
zsh's read-only 'status' variable crashed two separate background CI-watch scripts silently with exit code 1, requiring debugging and re-running.
Codex was rate-limited on the first review attempt ('reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews'), adding a multi-hour delay before the review loop could begin.
CI branch coverage measured differently from local -p runs (cross-crate --workspace boost); initially fixed coverage to 90.4% locally but had to verify with exact CI command to be confident.
Snapshot-count gate (≥1 snapshot per state variant) was not documented prominently; caught only by xci-fast, requiring an extra test+commit round for AngleSlider.
5 Codex review rounds with 22+ findings required ~5 push-test-fix-push cycles; several findings were cascading consequences of earlier fixes (e.g. mid-drag disable, SetProps reformatting).

color_area's apply_area_position with two sequential set_x/y_value calls — the second overwrote the first because both read from the same controlled color source. Required chaining both channels in one color read.

Rust stable 1.96 (released 3 days into the session) surfaced a pre-existing map_or_else lint in pagination/mod.rs under the workspace's deny(clippy::suspicious), requiring an unplanned fix to unblock CI.
open_items: Codex 👍 and user merge approval still pending at session end — PR #698 is CI-green but not yet merged; issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close on merge.
Adapter specs (leptos/dioxus-components/specialized/color-*.md) describe future framework-specific adapter components — explicitly out of scope per the issues, deferred to adapter tasks.

ColorValue type definition lives in spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md §Types (historical location); could be promoted to spec/shared/color-types.md for discoverability — orthogonal to correctness, not landed.

spec/foundation/04-internationalization.md MessageFn From-impl list is a representative sample, not exhaustive — the 4 new color-specific signatures (Fn(&ColorNameParts,&Locale), Fn(ColorChannel,&Locale), etc.) were intentionally not added to keep the listing consistent.

color_wheel and color_area lack a Label part but the spec references label IDs in some adapter examples — adapter implementers will need to decide on labelling strategy when building the adapter layer.
